config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menindas orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan dasar, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika hak individu dihormati, bukan dikalahkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, or government overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case; public safety, disease control, and basic law and order sometimes justify limits. The key point is that any restriction has to be narrow, necessary, and accountable, not a blank check for the majority. If society always wins, minorities and unpopular people are the first to get crushed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus lebih dulu daripada hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, entah terhadap kebebasan berpendapat, kelompok minoritas, atau warga kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti darurat kesehatan atau keamanan publik di mana hak bisa dibatasi, tetapi itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan dalih untuk menekan orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang benar-benar sehat justru yang melindungi hak individu karena dari situlah keadilan kolektif bisa tumbuh.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to justify discrimination, censorship, and abuses of power. At the same time, I do not see individual rights as unlimited, since things like public health, emergency response, and basic safety sometimes require real constraints. But those limits have to be specific, proportionate, and easy to challenge, or else “society” becomes just a cover for the majority to silence inconvenient people. To me, a healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and only narrows them when there is a clear, serious reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membungkam orang yang lemah atau tidak populer. Benar bahwa ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau ancaman keamanan yang bisa membatasi hak, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diawasi, bukan dijadikan kebiasaan. Kalau hak individu selalu dikorbankan atas nama kepentingan bersama, yang sering terjadi bukan kebaikan sosial, melainkan dominasi kelompok yang paling kuat. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu yang tegas, lalu kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa menginjak martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as a general rule, because from an American perspective it is too easy for “society first” to become a cover for mob rule, censorship, or discrimination against unpopular minorities. In the United States, we have a long history of people invoking the public good to justify abuses, so I am very wary of any principle that puts collective interests above individual rights by default. That said, I am not claiming rights are absolute in every situation; public health, emergency response, and safety can justify limits, but only when they are narrow, necessary, and accountable. To me, a free society protects the individual first and proves the public good through fair rules, not by crushing dissent or difference.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
